description: agents/firetiger.md has no YAML frontmatter block at all; the `name` field is absent, preventing proper agent registration in the plugin.
suggested_fix: Add a YAML frontmatter block at the top of the file with `name: firetiger`.

description: agents/firetiger.md has no YAML frontmatter block; the `description` field is absent, breaking agent discovery and help listings.
suggested_fix: Add `description: Firetiger observability platform context and available MCP tools reference` to the frontmatter block.

description: Agent has no YAML frontmatter and therefore no model field; the model tier is undeclared and will fall through to the runtime default.
suggested_fix: Add `model: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` or `model: claude-sonnet-4-6` to the frontmatter depending on desired capability tier.

description: Agent context document has no output format section; there is no specification of how the agent should structure its responses.
suggested_fix: Add an `## Output Format` section that describes the expected response structure (e.g., summary, query results, recommended actions).

description: Command frontmatter has no `allowed-tools` declaration; relies on MCP tools only but omitting the field leaves tool access unrestricted.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: []` if only MCP tools are intended, or list explicit built-in tools if Bash or Write are needed.

description: Phrase `until the agent is fully configured` uses vague completion criterion; `fully` is not defined.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete criterion such as `until the planner returns a conversation with no pending questions and the agent status is active`.

description: Command writes source files (tracing.ts, tracing.py, etc.) and updates .gitignore but declares no `allowed-tools`; Write and Edit tools are used without being listed.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Write, Edit, Bash]` to the frontmatter.

description: Step 2 says `Quickly identify the primary framework` but provides no bound on what constitutes a quick scan.
suggested_fix: Replace `Quickly identify` with `Identify` and specify the check order (e.g., `check package.json first, then requirements.txt, then go.mod`).

description: Step 4 says `Add the minimal instrumentation needed` where `minimal` is undefined; Claude must infer what counts as minimal.
suggested_fix: Replace `minimal instrumentation` with the specific artifacts to create (e.g., `a single tracing bootstrap file and the required environment variables`).

description: Command uses only MCP tools but has no `allowed-tools` declaration, leaving tool access unrestricted.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: []` to signal that only MCP tools are needed.

description: Investigation create fields include `services: Relevant services to investigate` where `relevant` is undefined.
suggested_fix: Replace `Relevant services` with `Services identified in Step 1 that produced errors or latency in the time window`.

description: Investigation create fields include `description: Detailed context about the issue` where `detailed` is undefined.
suggested_fix: Replace `Detailed context` with a concrete checklist (e.g., `include: symptom, first observed timestamp, affected services, recent deployments`).

description: Command uses Bash (gh CLI) explicitly but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash]` to the frontmatter.

description: Description says Firetiger `develops a targeted monitoring plan` where `targeted` has no defined criteria.
suggested_fix: Remove the adjective or specify: `a monitoring plan focused on the endpoints and services changed in the PR diff`.

description: Command uses only the MCP `query` tool but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: []` to signal MCP-only tool access.

description: Command is a SQL reference guide with no section specifying how query results should be formatted and presented to the user.
suggested_fix: Add an `## Output Format` section: e.g., `Present results as a markdown table with column headers. Summarize row count and any notable patterns before the table.`

description: Command uses Bash extensively (curl, aws, gcloud, wrangler, open/xdg-open) and writes files but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash, Write, Edit]` to the frontmatter.

description: Goal statement says `minimal user interaction` with no threshold defined for what counts as minimal.
suggested_fix: Replace with `at most one confirmation prompt per major action (instrumentation, connection, agent creation)`.

description: Section heading `Proactively connect` does not define what proactive means or when the agent should act without asking.
suggested_fix: Replace heading with `Automatically initiate (no confirmation needed)` and keep the existing parenthetical `(detected in Step 3)`.

description: Warning condition `if only some connections were skipped` uses vague `some`; quantity is unspecified.
suggested_fix: Replace with `if any connection attempt was skipped or failed` for a precise trigger.

description: Note says `Only ask for confirmation if genuinely uncertain` where `genuinely` provides no actionable decision rule.
suggested_fix: Replace with `Only ask for confirmation when two or more equally valid options exist and the choice cannot be inferred from the codebase`.

description: MCP server is a remote HTTP endpoint (https://api.cloud.firetiger.com/mcp/v1). All tool calls including get_ingest_credentials and agent management traverse this external connection. No credentials are hardcoded; trust depends on TLS and the vendor's server security.
suggested_fix: Document in the plugin README that all telemetry credentials are retrieved over HTTPS from the vendor endpoint; recommend users verify TLS certificate pinning is active.

description: Command instructs Claude to run `gh pr comment <PR_NUMBER> --body "@firetiger <monitoring context>"` where monitoring context is assembled from user input and PR diff. Unescaped shell metacharacters in user-provided text could break out of the --body argument boundary.
suggested_fix: Instruct Claude to write the comment body to a temp file and use `gh pr comment $PR_NUMBER --body-file /tmp/ft-comment.txt` to avoid shell interpolation.

description: Command instructs Claude to run `open "URL"` or `xdg-open "URL"` with a URL from the get_checkout_url MCP tool. If the MCP server ever returns a non-https scheme (file://, javascript:, etc.), the OS handler could execute unintended content.
suggested_fix: Add a guard before running open/xdg-open: `if [[ "$URL" != https://* ]]; then echo "Unexpected URL scheme, refusing to open"; fi`

description: Agent context lists supported MCP resource collections (agents, known-issues, connections, runbooks, triggers) but omits `investigations`, which is the primary resource used by commands/investigate.md.
suggested_fix: Add `investigations` to the Resources > CRUD operations list in agents/firetiger.md so the agent context is aware of the collection.

description: commands/query.md, commands/investigate.md, and commands/create-agent.md have no auth-fallback guard. If the Firetiger MCP server is not authenticated, these commands will fail silently. Only commands/setup.md handles the unauthenticated case.
suggested_fix: Add a brief Step 0 to each command: `If MCP tools are unavailable, direct the user to run /firetiger:setup first.`
